Petition For Approval An Dauthority To Compromise Heirship Dispute
Off Calendar Jan-11-2010 Granted Per Written Order

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order Issuing Commissions To Take Deposition In Foreign Counrty And To Allow The Depositions To Be Taken By Video Conferencing, Internet Conferencing Or Telephone Conference Call.
Off Calendar Dec-15-2009 Continued To Feb-02-10 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances, Petitioners Billy Eng, Richard Eng, Albert Ng and Mabel Hom's motion for order issuing commission to take deposition in Foreing County and allow deposition to be taken by video conference, per request of the moving party is continued to February 2, 2010 at 10:30 a.m. in Department 610. Commissioner: Paul Slavit. Not reported.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Petition To Determine Entitlement To Estate Distribution
Off Calendar Nov-16-2009 Continued To Jan-11-10 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Adriana Quintero for Jason Lee. Warren Woo for Billy Eng, Richard Eng, Albert Ng and Mable Hom.

Mr. Woo stated that discovery had not been completed, that he had 6 depositions to go and that Ms. Quintero still wanted to take some depositions. Ms. Quintero stated that there was no evidence of a legal adoption and that a discovery cut off date would be appropriate. Mr. Woo requested 60 days. The matter was continued to January 11, 2010 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge Pro Temp: Sue M. Kaplan. Reporter: Arlene Howell, CSR#2542. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy J. Capozzoli.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Petition To Determine Entitlement To Estate Distribution
Off Calendar Aug-24-2009 Continued To Nov-16-09 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Warren Woo for Billy Eng. Adrianne Quintero for Jason Lee.

Ms. Quintero reported that mediation was unsuccessful, stated that discovery needed to be conducted and that a trial date needed to be set. Mr. Woo stated that he needed at least 6 months to complete discovery, that there was an adoption that occurred in China over 80 years ago, that he needed to take depositions in Asia, that he obtained more information and that he had witnesses. Ms. Quintero objected to a 6 month period to do discovery and stated that she anticipated 6 weeks to complete the process. The Court stated that this case required documentary proof, that the adoption record did not exist and continued the matter to November 16, 2009 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for status on discovery. The Court requested Counsel file due diligence declarations with regard to the Heirs on the maternal side one week prior the hearing date.

Judge Pro Temp: Sue M. Kaplan. Reporter: LaVena Ward, CSR#7077. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy J. Gomez.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status Conference Following Mediation
Off Calendar Aug-24-2009 Text Ruling

Petition To Determine Entitlement To Estate Distribution
Off Calendar Jun-29-2009 Text Ruling

Petition To Determine Entitlement To Estate Distribution
Off Calendar Jun-22-2009 Continued To Jun-29-09 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Warren T. Woo for Billy Eng, Richard Eng, Albert Eng, and Mabel Lee Hom. Stephen Weinkauf for the San Francisco Public Administrator (SFPA). Adriana Quintero for Jason Chiu Ling Lee.

Mr. Weinkauf stated that they have looked for heirs and that he could file a declaration of due diligence regarding the search by the end of this week. Ms. Quintero stated that her clients would participate in mediation. Mr. Woo stated that he believed his clients would agree to mediate but he needed to check with one client. The Court stated that it would continue the matter one week, that if both sides agree to mediate then the Court would sign the order, no appearances would be required, and the order would be available on June 30, 2009 for pick up. The matter is to be referred to pro-bono mediation on June 29, 2009. The mediation is to be completed by August 14, 2009, and status on the mediation is set for August 24, 2009. The SFPA is to file their declaration of due diligence regarding their search for heirs. Discussions regarding discovery were held. The matter was continued to June 29, 2009 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204; no appearances are necessary unless the parties do not agree to mediate.

Judge Pro Temp: Sue M. Kaplan. Reporter: Susan Lee, CSR#4280. Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda. Bailiff: Deputy Sum.

REPORT OF SALE AND PETITION TO CONFIRM SALE OF REAL PROPERTY (30 Prescott Court, San Francisco, Ca 94133)
Off Calendar May-04-2009 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Steven Weinkauf for the Petitioner.

Mr. Weinkauf stated that he received written waivers from both Buyers. Baron Suen and Fanny Suen confirmed that they were waiving notice. The sale was confirmed at $615,000.00. No bond is required. The Court granted the petition, (subject to a written order).

Judge Pro Temp: Sue M. Kaplan. Reporter: Susan Lee, CSR#4280. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy M. Svahlin.
